title: LLP Chat Delegation Request
description: Payload that llp-chat-followup sends when delegating to a sub-agent. Mirrors Arc-Ai-style team-lead delegation. Phase A: synchronous (followup blocks on sub-agent response). Phase B (future): may add async streaming via message_update NDJSON event.

target_agent.description: Sub-agent to invoke. Must be present in followup's allowed delegate targets.

intent.description: Why followup is delegating. Free-form but normalized. Examples: verify_section_citation, verify_penalty_amount, verify_timeline, verify_eligibility, generate_termination_notice, generate_offer_letter, generate_grievance_letter, generate_show_cause.

claim.description: Required when target_agent=llp-chat-verify. The single specific factual claim followup wants source-checked. One sentence, normalized to English. Do NOT include followup's reasoning — verify needs the bare claim.

expected_section.description: Optional hint for verify when followup has a guess. Format: 'DOC-### §N' (e.g. 'DOC-010 §26', 'DOC-011 §27'). Verify confirms or refutes — does not blindly trust.

conversation_context.description: Last N turns of the conversation as plain strings, oldest-first. Each item formatted as 'User: ...' or 'Assistant (turn-1): ...' or 'Assistant (turn-2 followup): ...'. Used by sub-agent to ground the delegation; do not pad — keep tight.

document_request.description: Required when target_agent=llp-chat-filegen. Conforms to the filegen response-schema's input contract; see chat-proxy/data/llp-response-schema (filegen session output).

document_type.description: e.g. termination_notice, offer_letter, grievance_letter, show_cause, leave_application, custom

tier.description: 1=LLP legal canon, 2=generic HR/business, 3=arbitrary. Filegen session defines the tier policy.

requested_by.description: Identifies the lead agent. Sub-agents may use this to gate behavior (e.g. verify only accepts requests from followup, not from end users directly).

trace_id.description: End-to-end trace ID. Generated by chat-proxy at turn-2 entry, propagated through delegation chain, surfaced in logs/citations_audit for debugging.

max_response_ms.description: Soft timeout for sub-agent. Phase A: followup blocks up to this duration; if exceeded, followup proceeds without verification and surfaces 'unverified' badge. Phase B: timeout fires the async fallback.

examples.claim: Termination notice period for permanent workers under the 2026 Bangladesh Labour Amendment Act is 120 days.
conversation_context: User: What is the new notice period under the 2026 amendment?
Assistant (turn-1): The 2026 Bangladesh Labour (Amendment) Act extended the notice period for permanent workers to 120 days, per DOC-011 §27.
User: Are you sure it is 120 and not 60? My HR said 60.

examples.claim: Penalty for non-payment of provident fund under DOC-007 is BDT 25,000.
conversation_context: User: What happens if employer doesn't pay PF on time?
Assistant (turn-1): Employer faces a penalty under the Bangladesh Labour Rules 2015...
User: how much is the penalty exactly?
User: Draft a termination notice for a permanent worker, 6 months tenure, performance-based.
Assistant (turn-1): Under DOC-010 §26 and DOC-011 §27, you'll need to issue a notice with the following structure...

examples.claim: A worker with 4 months tenure is eligible for severance pay under DOC-010.
conversation_context: User: Is a 4-month employee entitled to severance?
Assistant (turn-1): Eligibility for severance under §26 of the 2006 Act requires...
